steps.explanation: This is one of the most common words in English. We see it in almost every sentence.

steps.instruction: Try to sound it out: t-h-e. Does it follow normal rules?
steps.work: t-h-e → /th/ + /e/? → That does not sound right!
steps.explanation: The word 'the' does not follow simple sounding-out rules. The 'e' at the end makes a /uh/ sound, not a short /e/. That is why we learn it as a sight word.

steps.instruction: Since 'the' is tricky, we memorize what it looks like. Spell it: t-h-e.
steps.explanation: Sight words are words we learn to recognize instantly without sounding them out. The word 'the' appears so often that fast recognition helps us read smoothly.

title: Reading Sight Words in a Sentence

steps.instruction: Look at this sentence: I do like you.
steps.explanation: This sentence has four words. Some can be sounded out and some are sight words.

steps.instruction: Which words are sight words? Sight words are common words we memorize.
steps.explanation: 'I', 'do', and 'you' are all kindergarten sight words. The word 'you' is tricky because the 'ou' does not make the sound you might expect.

steps.instruction: Now read the full sentence using your sight word knowledge.
steps.explanation: When you know sight words quickly, you can read sentences smoothly. Recognizing 'I', 'do', and 'you' right away lets you focus on the word 'like'.
